body_txt: So you've just given birth to a child and are not sure what to call them. Well, why not those you consider near and dear for a name? Usually, this trope involves someone deciding to name their child after a living person who they admire, love, or are closely acquainted with. This usually comes in the form of a friend, admirer, living relative, co-worker, or similar. This is generally used to show that the parent respects the other person enough that they decide to name their child after them. It's not uncommon for this to be a response to the other person helping out with the delivery and afterwards as well. Additionally, it commonly comes about as a result of a Last-Minute Baby Naming . Compare Ancestral Name , Dead Guy Junior , We Named the Monkey "Jack" , and Child Naming Request . Contrast Named After Somebody Famous . Sub-Trope to Meaningful Name and Significant Name Overlap . It also serves well as a way of averting One-Steve Limit . If a creator decides to do this in a meta way, that is Tuckerisation .
